claim: Object properties are forgotten separately rather than as integrated units over time.
Negative emotional responses drive behavior propagation more than positive ones despite equal initial magnitude.
Retrieval practice impairs performance when task structure makes item-level relations diagnostic, reversing the typical positive testing effect.
Unconscious processing outperforms conscious deliberation for complex multi-cue judgments by avoiding attentional bottlenecks and biases.
Patterns of consonantal articulation induce affect via simulation of ingestion versus expulsion kinematics, influencing preference for otherwise meaningless stimuli.
Acts motivated by communal obligation are perceived as less moral because obligation is inferred as the true goal, making helping seem incidental.
